description: warmstart: VLM Bias best (alpha=8.0 eps=0.5 gen-only layers 8-15 head_top_k=0.20) — this IS the baseline
alpha=0 eps=0 true no-op — identical to warmstart: gen-only boost does NOTHING on single-token A/B decisions
srf_apply_phase=both — +0.020! prefill boost critical for MMVP single-token decisions
srf_apply_phase=prefill — same as both (generation phase irrelevant for 1-token output)
alpha=12.0 phase=both — regression -0.027, too strong distorts attention
alpha=4.0 phase=both — +0.013 over alpha=8.0; gentler boost better for prefill
alpha=2.0 — slightly worse (63 vs 64 pairs); sweet spot at alpha=4.0
alpha=4.0 eps=0.2 — +0.007 (65 pairs); lower suppression sweet spot for MMVP
alpha=4.0 eps=0.0 — worse (63 pairs); mild suppression (0.2) better than none
layers 8-19 wider — big regression back to baseline; too many late layers interfere
layers 8-12 narrow — worse (61 pairs)
layers 8-14 — worse than 8-15 (62 vs 65); 8-15 is optimal layer range
head_top_k=0.10 — fewer heads worse (61 pairs)
head_top_k=0.30 — more heads worse (58 pairs)
clip_top_k_pct=0.20 — identical (65 pairs); saliency granularity insensitive
prob_interp — big regression to baseline
global_redistribute img_scale=2.0 — slight gain over baseline but worse than additive
clip_fallback_thresh=0.30 — worse; CLIP useful even at moderate confidence
clip_fallback_thresh=0.10 — same (65 pairs); threshold not sensitive
alpha=5.0 — same (65 pairs); alpha=4.0-5.0 are equivalent ceiling
sys_beta=0.0 — slightly worse (63 pairs); small sys suppression (0.10) is helpful
